sections.content:
Now I can see the full layout clearly:
Left side objects:
1. Rows 18-23: Gray block (6x6) with yellow 2x2 inside at center-right, with blue 4x2 block to its left
2. Rows 30-33: Gray block (4x4) + Red block (6x4) + dark red block (4x4) - horizontal strip
3. Rows 34-37: Scattered blue/charcoal 1-pixel dots forming a diagonal pattern
4. Rows 38-43: Player - gray 6x6 block with green 2x2 inside
Right side:
1. Red outlined shape (white border with red fill)
2. Blue outlined shape (white border with blue fill)
The player moved UP 3 times. Let me check the current position and see if it's approaching the scattered dots or the red block.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check player position after UP moves
seg = current_frame.segmentation
player = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['id'] == 32][0]
print(f"Player boundary: {player['boundary']}")

# Show area around player now
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nAround player (rows 32-44, cols 6-16):")
for r in range(32, 44):
    print(f"  {r:2d}: {lines[r][6:17]}")

# Check if any objects were absorbed/changed
print(f"\nPlayer pixels now: {player['pixels']}")
green = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['id'] == 34]
if green:
    print(f"Green child pixels: {green[0]['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
